ai_strategy_lead.name: Mitchell S. McKinney
ai_strategy_lead.title: Provost and Executive Vice President for Academic Affairs (convener of AI Task Force)

documents.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) at UToledo — strategic framework (Office of the Provost)

ai_strategy.summary: UToledo's provost-led AI strategy organizes work around three pillars: teaching and learning (discipline-specific AI skills for every student, plus faculty/staff professional development), research and innovation (establishing AI research hubs and interdisciplinary collaboration), and campus operations (AI for admissions, advising, IT, and smart facilities). Implementation is staged — identify early adopters, pilot at small scale, assess, then scale university-wide. The strategy is executed through an AI Task Force convened by the Provost with six subcommittees (arts/humanities, curriculum/instruction, healthcare, instructional development, policy, research).

key_provisions: Optional syllabus language provided to instructors; faculty set course-level permissibility
Assignment descriptions must contain explicit AI usage instructions
College of Law: generative AI cannot compose submitted work and is barred from exam use; research and grammar uses allowed
Library video series covers responsible use, evaluation of AI output, privacy/ethics/copyright
ai_policy.summary: UToledo takes a permissive-with-guidance approach: institution-wide guidance directs faculty to set AI rules at the course and assignment level with optional syllabus language, while specific units (notably the College of Law) publish stricter default rules against plagiaristic uses and exam use. The Policy subcommittee of the AI Task Force, chaired by Mark Halsey, examines legal and ethical issues. There is no single campus-wide prohibitive ban.

bodies.name: University of Toledo Artificial Intelligence Task Force
bodies.scope: Campus-wide AI governance convened by the Provost; six subcommittees on arts/humanities, curriculum and instruction, healthcare, instructional development, policy, and research

bodies.name: AI Steering Committee
bodies.scope: Examines AI-based tools and resources to enhance faculty, student and research staff capabilities

governance_structure.summary: Governance is centered on the Provost-convened AI Task Force (co-chaired by Gerald Natal and Arjun Sabharwal of University Libraries) with core members from the Division of Technology and Advanced Solutions (CIO Bill McCreary), Provost's office (Scott Molitor, Angela Paprocki), and Legal Affairs (Janelle Schaller). Six subcommittees cover arts/humanities (Olman Piedra), curriculum and instruction (Liang Cheng), healthcare, instructional development (Kimberly Fahey), policy (Mark Halsey), and research (Rick Francis). A separate AI Steering Committee examines tools and resources.

initiatives.title: AI Task Force with six specialized subcommittees
initiatives.description: Provost-convened task force establishing institution-wide AI governance across six domains: arts and humanities, curriculum and instruction, healthcare, instructional development, policy (legal/ethical), and research. Co-chaired by Provost Faculty Fellows Gerald Natal and Arjun Sabharwal.
initiatives.significance: Illustrates a distributed, domain-specific governance model being adopted by emerging R1s: rather than a single AI council, Toledo assigns sub-domains to faculty leads to produce practice-specific norms.

investment.description: Internal faculty/administrative time; no disclosed dollar amount

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a permanent governance structure with named subcommittee chairs for each major AI policy domain.

initiatives.title: Campus-wide Microsoft Copilot deployment for all employees
initiatives.description: UToledo has made Microsoft Copilot available to all university employees as part of a campus-wide culture of innovation. Faculty, staff, and administrators can use the integrated Windows 11 / Edge generative AI assistant for productivity, communication and content creation.
initiatives.significance: Places Toledo among the cohort of mid-sized R1 publics that have moved from ChatGPT-free-tier ambiguity to a licensed, enterprise-grade generative AI platform for workforce.

investment.funding_source: Division of Technology and Advanced Solutions
investment.description: Enterprise Microsoft 365 Copilot licensing (specific cost not disclosed)

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Productivity AI assistant available to all employees

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides a sanctioned enterprise LLM path, reducing reliance on personal-account ChatGPT/Claude usage.

initiatives.title: Nabla ambient AI clinical documentation scaled across UToledo Health
initiatives.description: UToledo Health, the academic health system serving northwest Ohio, selected Nabla after evaluating multiple ambient AI documentation assistants and scaled deployment to hundreds of physicians and advanced practice providers across primary care, surgery and other specialties. During an 8-week evaluation, clinicians cut time-to-chart-closure by 29% and reduced departmental backlogs from 400+ open charts to under 30.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates an R1 academic health system moving from ambient-AI pilot to at-scale Epic-integrated deployment with measurable revenue-cycle outcomes — a reference point for peer AMCs.

investment.description: Commercial SaaS license scaled from 8-week evaluation to hundreds of providers

collaborations.description: Ambient AI vendor deployed inside the Epic EHR

initiatives.policy_implications: Requires HIPAA-compliant handling of clinical encounters and integration with health-system documentation policy.

initiatives.title: Doug Reynolds — first AI virtual engagement officer at the UToledo Foundation
initiatives.description: UToledo Foundation partnered with Boston-based Givzey (Version2.ai) to deploy Doug Reynolds, an autonomous AI virtual engagement officer that interacts with alumni, donors, and community members about events, engagement opportunities and giving. The deployment subsequently became the first time autonomous fundraising technology secured multi-year gift commitments from donors.
initiatives.significance: An early example of AI moving into the advancement office at a US R1 — autonomous rather than assistive AI in donor relations.
initiatives.policy_implications: Raises disclosure questions about AI-mediated donor communications; the Foundation offers opt-in access.

initiatives.title: Toledo Athletics mandates AI training for entire department
initiatives.description: Toledo Athletics announced a first-in-the-nation requirement that all coaches, staff, and administrators complete artificial intelligence training as part of its Rise Together strategic vision. The program covers responsible AI use across athletic operations, performance, and business functions.
initiatives.significance: Positions an FBS athletic department as an early adopter of mandatory AI literacy — an unusual signal for a non-academic unit at an R1.

investment.description: Mandatory training curriculum; cost not disclosed

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a de facto AI AUP for an entire operational unit, tied to job expectations.

initiatives.title: School of Interdisciplinary Data Science established
initiatives.description: In spring 2025, UToledo launched the School of Interdisciplinary Data Science in the College of Natural Sciences and Mathematics, directed by Dr. Bill Kalies. The school brings together faculty from five colleges (Arts and Letters, Engineering, Health and Human Services, Natural Sciences and Mathematics, Education) and houses a BS in Data Science, a BA in Data Analytics, minors, graduate certificates, and an in-development online MS in Data Science.
initiatives.significance: Represents the kind of interdisciplinary restructuring R1s are using to concentrate AI/ML talent without creating a standalone AI school — leveraging existing strengths across colleges.

investment.funding_source: UToledo / College of Natural Sciences and Mathematics
investment.description: New academic unit with cross-college joint appointments

collaborations.description: Data Science Internship Program with Toledo Athletics for real-world applications

initiatives.title: AAC&U Institute on AI in Pedagogy and Curriculum (IAIPC) participation
initiatives.description: Five UToledo faculty (Leon Cheng, Jeanne Kusina, Jerry Natal, Arjun Sabharwal, Scott Molitor) represent UToledo in a year-long AAC&U Institute cohort aimed at improving faculty and staff professional development on AI and ensuring students meet discipline-specific AI learning outcomes.
initiatives.significance: Cross-institution faculty development cohort model — UToledo plugs into the AAC&U national network rather than building its own standalone AI pedagogy institute.

investment.description: Institute participation fees and faculty time

collaborations.description: National AAC&U cohort on AI in pedagogy and curriculum

initiatives.policy_implications: Produces local AI pedagogy guidance derived from AAC&U framework.

initiatives.title: Great Lakes AI Week sponsorship and host campus (Nov 4-7, 2025)
initiatives.description: UToledo was a sponsor and host of Great Lakes AI Week 2025, running JobsOhio-sponsored workshops (AI-Empowered Professional; AI Strategy & Governance), a sports & AI panel with Cleveland Cavaliers AI architect Ben Levicki, and an AI in Healthcare Symposium featuring senior health-system leadership (Charles Callahan, Scott Molitor, Imran Ali) and distinguished professor Bina Joe.
initiatives.significance: Anchors UToledo as the regional hub for AI ecosystem development in northwest Ohio — connects university to JobsOhio and regional industry.

investment.description: Multi-event regional AI week; specific sponsorship level not disclosed

collaborations.description: Regional state-economic-development partner plus industry association plus neighboring R2

initiatives.title: AI in Healthcare Symposium — AI/ML microbiome research
initiatives.description: Dr. Bina Joe, Distinguished University Professor, presented 'Leveraging AI/Machine Learning to Access Microbiomes as Risk Factors for Disease' at the Nov 7, 2025 symposium co-hosted by the College of Medicine and Life Sciences, UToledo Health, and the Office of Continuing Medical Education.
initiatives.significance: Showcases how an R1 academic medical center is blending wet-lab biomedical research with ML-based pattern discovery — a key vector for NIH/NSF AI-for-science funding.

investment.funding_source: College of Medicine and Life Sciences / UToledo Health
investment.description: Faculty research program, exact funding not disclosed

collaborations.description: Internal research; specific external partners not disclosed

initiatives.title: TechCred-funded AI certificate programs (business, marketers, ChatGPT, Copilot)
initiatives.description: Through UToledo's Family Business Center, the university offers state-funded (Ohio TechCred) continuing-education certificates in AI for Business, AI for Marketers, ChatGPT, and Microsoft 365 Copilot — targeting regional workforce upskilling.
initiatives.significance: Illustrates a regional R1 leveraging state workforce funding to deliver short-cycle AI upskilling to working adults, independent of degree programs.

collaborations.description: State-funded certificate pathway using vendor platforms

initiatives.title: AI specialization in graduate Electrical Engineering & Computer Science
initiatives.description: The EECS graduate program offers an Artificial Intelligence specialization covering machine learning, data mining, fuzzy logic and reasoning, heuristic search, optimization, adaptation and automation, and probabilistic modeling.
initiatives.significance: Long-standing academic AI track feeding graduate-level AI workforce and research — a stable spine for the university's newer AI initiatives.

initiatives.title: Ohio Supercomputer Center partnership for AI/ML research
initiatives.description: UToledo researchers use the statewide Ohio Supercomputer Center (OSC) for AI/ML research (e.g., Dr. Anju R. Gupta's lab applies ML and statistical forecasting to predict desalination-membrane lifespans). Historically, OSC awarded a dedicated cluster to UToledo's Department of Physics and Astronomy.
initiatives.significance: Toledo, like many state-system R1s, leans on a shared state HPC asset rather than building its own AI supercomputer — a model for cost-efficient AI compute access.

investment.funding_source: State of Ohio / Ohio Supercomputer Center

initiatives.title: AI LibGuide and video literacy series from University Libraries
initiatives.description: UToledo Libraries publishes a comprehensive AI LibGuide plus a six-video literacy series covering responsible use, evaluation of AI output, privacy/ethics/copyright, research writing, and prompting, supporting students and faculty across disciplines.
initiatives.significance: Library-led AI literacy is a common early-maturity pattern — important baseline for compliance with course-level AI disclosure expectations.
initiatives.policy_implications: Embeds responsible-use expectations at the student research level.

research_centers.name: School of Interdisciplinary Data Science
research_centers.focus: Interdisciplinary data science research, education and innovation; ML/AI applications across five colleges

ai_academic_programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Data Science
Master of Science in Data Science (in development)
Cyber Security and Trust graduate specialization (includes AI-for-cybersecurity)

computing_infrastructure.summary: UToledo does not operate a dedicated central AI supercomputer; instead, researchers access the state-shared Ohio Supercomputer Center (OSC) for large-scale AI/ML workloads. Historically OSC awarded a Cluster Ohio node to UToledo's Department of Physics and Astronomy.

investments_and_gifts.title: Research funding (FY2024 external awards)
investments_and_gifts.source: Competitive external grants (federal, industry, philanthropy combined)
investments_and_gifts.purpose: All research (AI activity funded within this envelope)

industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: Enterprise Microsoft 365 Copilot deployment and TechCred Copilot certificate
Ambient AI clinical documentation at UToledo Health
Great Lakes AI Week workshops (AI-Empowered Professional; AI Strategy & Governance)
Great Lakes AI Week regional programming (Nov 5-6, 2025)
Institute on AI in Pedagogy and Curriculum cohort
State-shared HPC access for AI/ML research
Academic Affiliation Agreement for medical education and clinical research (context for health-system AI deployments)

student_facing_ai.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot for students/staff
student_facing_ai.scope: Campus-wide employee access; students access via M365 licenses

student_facing_ai.name: Doug Reynolds (alumni/donor engagement)
student_facing_ai.scope: Alumni, donors, community — not current-student facing, but campus-community facing

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Library AI literacy video series (free, open)
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Free, short-form videos from UToledo Libraries on responsible AI use, aimed at closing AI-literacy gaps for students, faculty, and community members regardless of technical background.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Ohio TechCred-funded AI certificates
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: State-subsidized AI certificate programs (AI for Business, AI for Marketers, ChatGPT, Copilot) available at low or no cost to employed learners in Ohio through the Ohio TechCred reimbursement program.

regulatory_context.summary: Ohio enacted HB 96, which required the Ohio Department of Education and Workforce to publish a model AI policy for K-12 by Dec 31, 2025 (published Dec 30, 2025) and all traditional districts / community schools / STEM schools to adopt formal AI policies by July 1, 2026. HB 96 also authorizes an AI Integration in Community Colleges Grant Program (five $100,000 annual awards to community colleges, technical colleges and state institutions). No single statewide AUP governs Ohio R1 universities; UToledo is not a member of a university system and sets its own campus AI policy. Ohio higher-ed governance is advised by the Inter-University Council of Ohio (CIOs committee).

narrative_summary: The University of Toledo — a public R1 (Carnegie 2025) serving ~14,400 students in northwest Ohio and unaffiliated with a larger state system — is operating a maturing, provost-led AI posture under recently arrived President James Paul Holloway (July 2025) and Provost Mitchell S. McKinney (July 2025). Governance runs through an Artificial Intelligence Task Force convened by the Provost, co-chaired by library Provost Faculty Fellows Gerald Natal and Arjun Sabharwal, with a core team that explicitly pulls in the CIO (Bill McCreary), Legal Affairs, and the Office of the Provost. Six named subcommittees (arts/humanities, curriculum/instruction, healthcare, instructional development, policy, research) distribute domain-specific work. Policy is permissive-with-guidance at the institution level — optional syllabus language, course-level faculty discretion — with stricter carve-outs such as the College of Law's generative-AI rule and the first-in-the-nation mandatory AI training rolled out to Toledo Athletics in June 2025. Enterprise deployments are where Toledo stands out: Microsoft 365 Copilot is live for all employees, UToledo Health has scaled Nabla ambient AI to hundreds of clinicians on Epic (29% reduction in time-to-chart-closure), and the UToledo Foundation deployed Givzey's Doug Reynolds, an autonomous AI virtual engagement officer, in May 2025. Academically, the School of Interdisciplinary Data Science (spring 2025) pulls together faculty across five colleges with an existing BS and an in-development online MS; AI specializations sit inside graduate EECS, and Ohio TechCred funds a portfolio of continuing-ed AI certificates. Research compute is outsourced to the statewide Ohio Supercomputer Center, and the university sponsored Great Lakes AI Week (Nov 2025) with JobsOhio and Bowling Green State. Principal gaps: no chief AI officer, no disclosed AI-specific investment total, no named AI research institute, and the statewide higher-ed regulatory framework is still forming (HB 96's higher-ed provisions target community colleges). Overall posture is growing, operationally credible, and distinctly shaped by a health-system and regional-workforce agenda more than by a flagship research-AI brand.

gaps_acknowledged: No chief AI officer or named AI Strategy Lead beyond the Provost-as-convener; profile role is effectively shared between Provost McKinney and task-force co-chairs Natal/Sabharwal.
No disclosed total dollar investment specifically earmarked for AI (separate from the $72.2M FY2024 total research awards).
Policy adoption_date not publicly stated on the Provost AI policies page.
AI Steering Committee page returned only a directory listing to WebFetch — member list not fully captured; Task Force page provided the concrete membership data.
No evidence of a standalone AI research center with a dedicated institute name; data science school is the closest analogue.
No public AI-specific controversies or litigation located — may reflect genuine absence or limited public reporting.
Dedicated on-campus GPU cluster count not published; compute is primarily via Ohio Supercomputer Center.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: curriculum led.
